channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Manly apartment of ex-Olympian Ian Brown listed for $12.5 million with waterfront features
item.description: A rare waterfront apartment in Manly, Sydney, formerly owned by Olympian sailor Ian Brown, has been listed for sale with a price guide of $12.5 to $13.75 million. The property is described as a 'house...

item.title: Centre to Decide Speed Limits on National Highways, States to Manage Local Roads
item.description: The Ministry of Road Transport has proposed that the authority to set speed limits on National Highways (NHs) and expressways be centralised under the Centre, while state governments will continue to ...

item.title: Tanzania Defends Its Handling of 2025 Election Violence at UN Human Rights Council
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has strongly rejected what it calls biased and incomplete statements made by several international partners and human rights organisations concerning violence linked to the ...

item.title: London Council Approves 30-Storey Residential Tower on Ridout Street Despite Tenant Concerns
item.description: The London City Council has approved a plan for a 30-storey high-rise building on Ridout Street, which will include 1,500 residential units and 136 parking spaces. The decision comes amid concerns fro...

item.title: Tanzania Boxing Achieves Milestone with Certified Coaches and Referee
item.description: Tanzania amateur boxing has just scored a big milestone for its technical side. Over 40 local coaches now hold international certifications, which is a major shift since before, Tanzanian boxers had t...

item.title: MPs criticised for taking part in parliamentary dance event amid escalating Iran conflict
item.description: On 4 March 2026, more than 40 MPs participated in a cha-cha-cha dance session in Westminster's Portcullis House, organised to highlight the health and wellbeing benefits of dance. Strictly Come Dancin...

item.title: Zanzibar’s Kikwajuni Housing Project Gets Green Light, Residents Compensated
item.description: Residents of Kikwajuni area in Zanzibar have finally received Sh1.8 billion as the government resolves a long-standing dispute over housing redevelopment. The Zanzibar government, through the Zanzibar...

item.title: ADvTECH plans new 10 500-student Emeris mega-campus in Umhlanga, KZN, with first phase opening in 2029
item.description: ADvTECH, South Africa's leading private education provider, has announced plans for a major new mega-campus in Umhlanga, KwaZulu-Natal. The group has acquired 10 hectares of land next to the N2 highwa...

item.title: President Trump to Attend Dover Ceremony for Six Service Members Killed by Iranian Drone in Kuwait
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to attend the dignified transfer ceremony for six American service members killed in a deadly Iranian drone strike on a U.S. tactical operations center in a commercial por...

item.title: BJP Deliberates Carefully Before Choosing New National President
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), along with the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS), is taking a cautious approach in selecting a new national president. Even though the term of Jagat Prakash Nadda end...

item.title: PM Shehbaz launches five-year strategy to strengthen Pakistan’s flood management
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has approved a detailed five-year plan to improve Pakistan’s flood preparedness and climate resilience, announced Climate Minister Musadik Malik. This plan follows the he...

item.title: Minister David Umahi Rejects Social Media Claims of Harassment and Debt, Focuses on Infrastructure Delivery
item.description: Senator David Umahi, the Minister of Works, has strongly dismissed allegations made against him on social media by one Mrs Tracy Nither Ohiri. The claims reportedly involve accusations of sexual haras...

item.title: Woman finds suspected explosives on her farm in Isiolo and hands them to police
item.description: Residents of Kambi ya Juu village in Isiolo were left shaken after a 71-year-old woman, Severina Kairigo, brought suspected explosive devices she had found on her farm to the local police post. She di...

item.title: Punjab Forms Joint Investigation Teams to Probe TLP Cases
item.description: LAHORE: The Punjab government, led by Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz, has established 12 Joint Investigation Teams (JITs) to probe cases against the proscribed Tehreek-e-Labbaik Pakistan (TLP). Seven JIT...

item.title: Vice Mayor and Companions Face Charges in Misamis Oriental Woman's Killing
item.description: CAGAYAN DE ORO CITY, Philippines — A municipal vice mayor from Lanao del Sur, together with his companions, has been officially charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a woman and the injury ...

item.title: Australians safely repatriated from Middle East amid regional conflict
item.description: The first group of Australians stranded in the Middle East due to the escalating conflict have been successfully repatriated to Sydney, marking the beginning of a larger operation to bring home the re...

item.title: Inclusive design enhances societal resilience during crises
item.description: The article explores the concept of inclusive design and how designing with people who face barriers benefits everyone. It begins with the well-known 'curb-cut effect,' where small modifications to as...

item.title: Third Way Centrists Discuss 2028 Strategy Amid Iran War Divisions
item.description: Centrist Democrats gathered at a Third Way conference to strategize for the 2028 presidential election, focusing initially on domestic issues like affordability and elevating moderate voices online. T...

item.title: Madurai High Court allows more time for temple board to respond on Karthigai Deepam prayer arrangement
item.description: The Madurai Bench of the Madras High Court has granted additional time to the Board of Trustees of the Subramaniya Swamy Temple at Thirupparankundram to deliberate on a court suggestion related to the...

item.title: U.S.-Israeli Strikes Sink Iranian Warship Near Sri Lanka Amid Escalating West Asia Conflict
item.description: On 4th March 2026, a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship, IRIS Dena, in the Indian Ocean near Sri Lanka, resulting in the death of at least 83 people. U.S. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed ...
